item.title: Zellij
item.description: Scrolling Zellij support scrolling with mouse out of the box, and can auto-detect a scrollable program and switch from scrollback buffer to it. Sixel Escape CodeExplanationCSI c or CSI 0 cCSI is ESC [.

item.title: Blink-based SSH Workstation
item.description: Server Setup I use Nix to install latest software, chezmoi to manage configuration files, and starship and fastfetch for my shell (zsh). fd is installed as a dependency for Doom Emacs, and mosh is used with Blink to provide UDP roaming capability.

item.title: AWS CLB Surge Queue and Little's Law
item.description: Metrics from a TCP Listener SampleCount SampleCount of RequestCount and Latency metrics are exactly the same as number of requests. SampleCount of SurgeQueueLength is roughly number of requests doubled.

item.title: a-Shell
item.description: Settings Settings are available in the system’s Settings app. Directories ~xxx represents a bookmark. You can list existing bookmarks with showmarks. ~ (home directory) is not writable, so there are pre-defined environment variables that redirect programs to use ~/Documents or ~/Library.

item.title: Best way to add path to PATH environment variable
item.description: Solution if [[ ! "$PATH" == *your_path* ]]; then PATH="${PATH:+${PATH}:}your_path" fi Explanation [[ ! "$PATH" == *your_path* ]] checks if PATH contains your new path already, hence avoiding duplicates.

item.title: Convenient CLI Tools
item.description: dog (DNS resolution) xhs, xh (HTTP/S request) Zellij (tmux) eza, lsd (ls) fd, bfs (find) rg (grep) pv (pipe viewer) dug (DNS propagation checker) ffsend (share files securely) age (simple file encryption tool) restic (file backup tool) rclone (rsync for cloud) namei (check parent directory permissions) ncdu (TUI disk usage analyzer) lft (Layer Four Traceroute) .

item.title: Killing inactive SSH Sessions on Server
item.description: w # find the TTY to kill who -u | grep pts/XXX # grep for TTY to find PID (last column) ps aux | grep <PID> # verify what the process is # If <PID> is an interactive shell, kill -HUP <PID> # Otherwise kill <PID> References bash(1) .

item.title: namei
item.description: namei -om <path> displays permissions follows each pathname and prints their modes and owners. <path> should be absolute.

item.title: yay
item.description: Install /usr/bin/core_perl must be added to PATH when you run yay. Re-login to fix pod2man not found issue. Clean Build Clean build is effectively git reset --hard origin/HEAD; git clean -fdx before building the package.

item.title: rust
item.description: With rust-analyzer, hyperlinks from markdown hover text (get-text-property (point) 'help-echo) are not clickable. This is supposedly fixed in Emacs (Eglot) commit cb562118cb14, but does not work with rustic specifically.
